mnemonics: Global states are the set of all possible states of a distributed system.
Think of a snapshot of the system at a particular time.
keyPoints: A global state is a unique combination of the states of all processes in the system.
Each process has a local state, and a global state is a combination of all local states.
In a distributed system, multiple processes can execute concurrently, leading to multiple possible global states.
To determine the global state of a distributed system, we need to consider the states of all processes.
A global state can be thought of as a snapshot of the system at a particular time, including the states of all processes.
